description_long: This is a vintage monoplane with a wooden frame and fabric-covered wings and tail surfaces, reminiscent of early 20th-century aviation designs. The fuselage is slender and exposed, revealing a latticework of wooden struts and spars, with a single open cockpit positioned near the front. The wings are straight and mounted high on the fuselage, supported by tension wires for structural integrity. The landing gear consists of two large spoked wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear, indicating a taildragger configuration. The engine is mounted at the nose, featuring a wooden propeller and visible engine components. The tail assembly is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all fabric-covered and supported by additional bracing wires.
description: A vintage, fabric-covered monoplane with a wooden frame, open cockpit, high straight wings, and exposed engine and landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the exposed wooden frame, high-mounted straight wing, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, symmetrical wing shape, and narrow fuselage with visible cockpit and engine at the front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail structure, high wing, and detailed landing gear with spoked wheels and tail skid.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the wooden propeller, open cockpit, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing gear and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight, unswept wings and external bracing wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers, all fabric-covered and externally braced.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main spoked wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit integrated into the front section of the fuselage, with no canopy or enclosed windows.
